name: Ab Wheel Rollout
resistance_profile: Anti-extension: as you roll out, the lever lengthens and the abs must resist the lumbar spine extending — load peaks at full extension, the deepest stretch.
cues: Ribs down, pelvis tucked the whole time
Roll only as far as you can keep the back flat
Pull yourself back with the abs, not the hip flexors
execution_steps: Kneel on a pad and grip the wheel on the floor under your shoulders with straight arms.
Brace the abs hard and tuck the pelvis so the lower back is flat, not arched.
Roll the wheel forward slowly, extending only as far as you can keep the spine from sagging.
Pause briefly at your end range without letting the lower back arch.
Pull with the abs to roll the wheel back to the start under control.
good_when: You want a loaded lengthened stretch for the abs
You want high-tension anti-extension work with progressive range
bad_when: You have a cranky or extension-sensitive lower back
You can't yet hold a solid braced plank position
common_errors: Letting the lower back sag or arch at full extension
Rolling out further than the abs can control
progressions: Roll out to a greater range
Progress from kneeling to standing rollouts
regressions: Limit range with a wall or box as a stop
